items.title: JPMorgan Stock Drops After Bank Predicts $105 Billion in Expenses Next Year
items.description: JPMorgan Chase’s stock fell more than 4.7% on Tuesday after the bank told investors that it will spend billions of dollars more in expenses next year.  At the Goldman Sachs conference in New York on Tuesday, consumer chief Marianne Lake told investors that the bank had just completed budgeting for next year and is planning to spend $105 billion, largely because of higher costs in her division.  The bank is expecting expenses this year to be $95.9 billion and analysts had anticipated approximately $101 billion of expenses next year, according to FactSet.

items.title: JPMorgan’s Stock Slide Weighs on Dow Industrials
items.description: The blue-chip index fell 0.4%, dragged down by JPMorgan Chase shares, ahead of the Fed’s rate decision. The S&P 500 slipped 0.1%, while the Nasdaq added 0.1%.

items.title: GameStop, JPMorgan Chase, AeroVironment,GE Vernova And Cracker Barrel: Why These 5 Stocks Are On Investors' Radars Today
items.description: U.S. stocks were mixed as traders positioned ahead of this week's Federal Reserve meeting, with major indexes consolidating near recent highs after a strong year-end run for value names.

items.title: Top Analyst Reports for JPMorgan Chase, EPAM & Costco
items.description: New analyst reports spotlight JPMorgan Chase as operational strength, NII prospects and cost pressures shape its outlook.

items.title: JPMorganChase Declares Common Stock Dividend
items.description: NEW YORK, December 09, 2025--JPMorgan Chase & Co. (NYSE: JPM) ("JPMorganChase" or the "Firm") declared a quarterly dividend on the outstanding shares of the common stock of JPMorganChase. Information can be found on the Firm’s Investor Relations website at https://www.jpmorganchase.com/ir/news.
